MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Andrew Schultz for Debra Dolch. Court-Appointed Counsel Franchesca Callejo for Winnifred B. Rawlings.

Ms. Callejo stated that they're here to support the request. The Court stated that there is no objection, that things appear to be in order, and that the request to confirm the investment decisions seeks the same language. Mr. Schultz presented argument in support of the petition stating that there is a fixed set of investments and no purchase of new securities, that they want to stay in the same portfolio, and that the investment advisor wants to be able to respond to the market. The Court stated that it had some concerns with the language. Ms. Callejo stated that the language is confusing. Mr. Schultz stated that at the next review that if there were accounts purchased that they want to make sure that it's understood that it is an authorized investment. The Court and counsel confer regarding the requested language in the order. The Court stated that things have been fine, that it understands that this was the language that was approved last time, but that the Court would like to see new language. The Fifth Account and Report of Trustee and Petition for Its Settlement; Petition for Allowance of Successor Trustee's Fees and Attorneys' Fees and Costs; Petition to Re-Approve Investments; and Petition to Reduce Bond is granted (subject to a revised order). Mr. Schultz is to prepare a revised form of order. Ms. Callejo noted that she would be glad to review the proposed order before it is submitted to the Court.

Petition For Instructions Regarding Repayment Of Loan
Off Calendar Dec-20-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for and with Debra Dolch.
Trisha Friedeberg for Anne Clare (Beneficiary).
Timothy Doyle appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

Mr. Siracusa stated that all of the other Children believe the notes were paid off because they all had similar notes that had been paid off, that his Client thought Anne had the original notes, but they now found out that the Parents never asked for the original notes, and that the only parties who had first hand knowledge are the Deceased Husband and the Wife. Mr. Siracusa also stated that there is no proof that any of the notes were paid off, that the note does not say compound interest is to be applied, and the Court can order pay off of the note with or without compound interest, or that it not be paid off. Ms. Friedeberg stated that having the original notes is not determinative of whether the notes were paid off, that it is her understanding that Ms. Rawlings gifted the Caravel property to all of the Children, but not to her Client as she expressed to her Mother that she did not want to be financially connected to her Siblings and that these notes would account for her Client's share only creating some parity. Ms. Friedeberg also stated that the only way there would be parity is if compound interest is paid on the notes and that otherwise, her Client would not get her share of the valuable property. Mr. Doyle referred to Ms. Clare's declaration in response to the restraining order, stated that Anne hired an Attorney to sever all financial ties with the Family, that the Father paid everybody off because Anne sent a demand for payment of the notes and that the Children were told to keep the letter for when Anne came after them. Ms. Friedeberg stated that she is not requesting an evidentiary hearing, that Ms. Dolch has seen no documentation in the time she has been appointed, that other notes are due in December of 2018 and March of 2019, and requested time to amend the petition to include those notes. Mr. Siracusa stated that he does not feel anything else can be presented and that they can address the other notes at a later time. The Petition for Instructions is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Ms. Freideberg requested a 2 week continuance to file a response, stating that she recently spoke with her client and just saw the papers yesterday. Mr. Siracusa stated that there is no evidence the promissory note be paid with compound interest, there is no evidence the note has been paid or not paid, the settlor has 6 children and all other notes have been paid. Mr. Siracusa stated he will submit an amended petition to state what they are asking for, and clarified that he never represented Anne Claire. ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-16-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PTR-10-294033 The Winnifred Rawlings Trust and PCN-10-294034 Conservatorship of Winnifred Rawlings.
Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for (conservator & trustee) Debra Dolch. Amy Harrington for (objector) Eileen Doten.

The Court stated that is has read the supplements. Mr. Siracusa stated that they hope they have addressed most of the objections, that they are assuming that the remaining issue is the care of the conservatee in San Francisco prior to the move, that they have not interjected into the Idaho issues, that it is a false narrative that Ms. Rawlings was receiving poor care in San Francisco, that they cared for her very well and were respectful of Ms. Rawlings wishes, that there were some charting errors regarding the restaurants, that they tried to keep Ms. Rawlings active and that they received an email from Ms. Callejo who is out with surgery indicating that she has no objections and supports the settlement. Ms. Harrington stated that the supplements did not respond to their concerns, that they need copies of the credit card receipts and charts, that Ms. Doten does not understand why they took her to Daly City and other strange entries, that Ms. Rawlings was in an accident while going to Daly City, that the supplement says the note to Michael Doten was not paid off, that they are not sure how the accounting could balance, that they want the opportunity to respond to the supplement and to gather more information, that Ms. Doten wants to appear telephonically at the next hearing and that they want the bank copies of the signed credit card receipts, statements and daily charts. Mr. Siracusa stated that it is troubling that accounting was filed in the trust matter 3 months ago but objections were not filed until last week, that they have not been paid for two years, that supplying the credit card statements is easy but the signed receipts will be difficult to obtain, that they would like the objections overruled, that the dispute is between the siblings and the aunt, that they can informally provide the information being requested, that if a charge is not made by the conservatee or for her benefit they will file a fraud report and that she is objecting to a note to the sister which was paid off years ago. Ms. Harrington stated that her client has a procedural right to file objections, that they tried to resolve their concerns informally, that Ms. Doten sent many emails to Ms. Dolch's office regarding the credit card with no response, that she wants to be heard in court and that there are discrepancies regarding the interest rates on loans. The Court took the Third Account and Report of Successor Trustee and the Amended second report of Conservator of Estate, etc., under submission as of August 15, 2016. Ms. Harrington has until August 8, 2016 to file an additional response and Mr. Siracusa is to make a best effort production of additional documents by August 3, 2016.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-21-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-10-294034 Conservatorship of Winnifred Rawlings.

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Debra Dolch. Franchesca Callejo for and with Winnifred Rawlings. Amy Harrington for Eileen Doten (Daughter of Conservatee/Benefciary). Berenice Parsons (Daughter of Conservatee) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Siraucusa presented oral argument, explained circumstances and work performed by Ms. Dolch to support her fee request. Mr. Siracusa also stated that had a meeting with Ms. Harrington, her Client and Timothy with regard to the Parties working more cooperatively and that they (Mr. Siracusa and Ms. Dolch) agreed to reduce their rates by 5%. In addition, Mr. Siracusa explained that they requested a lien on the Arguello property because that was the property that most likely would be sold, stated that the lien would be on Ms. Rawlings share/interest of the property, that there is no TIC agreement, that he can amend the petition to have the lien against the Van Ness property or that he can add language in the proposed order with regard to the other Owner's 50% interest not being affected by the lien. Ms. Callejo expressed her Client's concern about overall expenses. Furthermore, Mr. Siracusa reported that Ms. Parson and Chris Doyle were unable to make payments on their notes, that none of the notes are secured, that Timothy was making payments and then stopped, and that he asked Timothy to bring it current. Mr. Siracusa also stated that Kathy and Eileen are current but the problem with their notes is that they are due, that he asked them to allow (Siracusa/Dolch) to secure all of their notes on the San Jose Partnership property so that all of the notes are secured, that Chris agreed, that Eileen and Tim are inclined to agree if Berenice agrees to secure hers and asked the Court for direction on this issue and set status four months from now. Ms. Parson provided history with regard to the property she resides in known as "The Ranch", explained ownership, what the Mother contributed, loan amount ($399,000.00 note), payments (Mother at the very most contributed $10,000.00 down initially), stated that she had an issue with how Ms. Dolch was calculating interest (compound versus simple), that her note was secured by the Santa Rita property and that she would like to sell her property "The Ranch" and pay her Mother off. The Court would like a declaration regarding the TIC agreement (Arguello Property) and asked that the property (The Ranch) history be written out and provided to Mr. Siracusa. The Petition to Direct Filing of An 850 Petition and for Authorization to Pursue Non Paying Obligees on Notes was bifurcated and set for a status date/deadline of February 19, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to get everyone in the same position. The accountings in both matters were taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Ex Parte Petition To Bring Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision; To Appoint A Temporary Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-06-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-10-294034 Conservatorship of Winnifred B. Rawlings.
Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for and with Debra Dolch. Franchesca Callejo, Court appointment Counsel for Winnefred Rawlings (also present). Nancy Rasch for and with Kathleen Zdanky. Jeff Allen for and with Timothy Doyle, Eileen Doton and Bernice Parsons. Chris Doyle (Son) also present.

Counsel spoke of Ms. Rawlings capacity/incapacity to manage her finances or the influence of others, her information processing defecits, Ms. Rawlings being caught in the middle of all of the back and forth accusations between the Siblings, her ability/inability to keep track of the loans and the payments, the gifts, the sale of the San Jose property, the captial gains tax issue, whether a temporary conservatorship was needed/not needed and the appointment of Ms. Dolch or another neutral Fiduciary. The Court stated that it wanted to see a neuro psych evaluation report. The petition for temporary conservatorship of the estate was granted with a bond set at $1,600,000.00. The petition for temporary conservatorship of the person was denied without prejudice on a temporary basis. The hearing date on the general petition was continued to February 24, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If Mr. Allen's Clients file a competing petition, it is to be noticed for a hearing date of February 24, 2011. The petition to appoint a temporary Successor Trustee of the Trust was granted with bond set at $167,000.00. The hearing on the Trust matter will also be continued to February 24, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Rawlings will be allowed to maintain her own checking account with a balance of $5,000.00. The Court encouraged the Parties to consider mediation. The request to dispense with notice of the temporary conservatorship was granted.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Henry.
